description:
Advances: docs (requirement 5 of the P1)

1. DELETE every test_helper.exs formatter-wiring snippet: docs/adoption.md:131-144 and :292-313, skills/spec-led-bootstrap/SKILL.md:147, references/adoption-phases.md:156-172 (incl. the escape-hatch line), references/task-templates.md:303-327, references/detection-matrix.md:152, priv/spec_init/agents/skills/spec-led-development/SKILL.md.eex daily-loop step 7. Replace with: 'Setup is one command: mix spec.cover.test. Never add SpecLedEx.Coverage.Formatter anywhere — it is inert unless the task arms it.' Phase-4 detection heuristic: artifact-exists / CI-runs-cover instead of grep-for-Formatter. 2. Fix false 'mix spec.check runs triangulation' claims (docs/adoption.md:25-27,137-139,305-313,356-361,423-434; docs/concepts.md:259-265; scaffolded SKILL.md.eex finding table lines 30-31,58-61) — name the real consumers spec.triangle + spec.review (Decision 1). 3. NEW docs/coverage.md linked from a README command table listing both coverage commands: one-command entry; coveralls coexistence + mix spec.cover.ingest; what the numbers mean (percent = ANY-test; self-verified = additionally an executed tagged test); --per-test contract (serialized required, async fails, --allow-async degrades, measured cost model, scoping knobs); prerequisites (tracer manifest); hygiene (.gitignore .spec/_coverage/ and cover/, generated_at staleness); full refusal-reason catalogue with fixes. 4. priv/spec_init/workflows/spec_review.yml.eex: run mix spec.cover.test before mix spec.review. 5. 0.4.0 release notes lead with behavior breaks: test_helper wiring now no-ops loudly; spec.cover.test default changed (no serialization, envelope artifact, name kept per Decision 2); async during --per-test fails; legacy artifacts rejected (Decision 5).

## Orchestrator addenda (admission, epic @ 170c564)

All 8 code/spec tickets are merged — the docs must describe LANDED reality, verified against the tree, not the design doc. Ground truth: default 'mix spec.cover.test' = plain 'mix test --cover --export-coverage specled' + Aggregate.ingest to a v2 envelope (exit-code passthrough; non-zero + {:refused,...} sidecar on empty/refusal); 'mix spec.cover.ingest <path.coverdata>' = coveralls/CI escape hatch; '--per-test' = opt-in serialized lane on Snapshot native/classic engine (async hard-fail, --allow-async degrades); formatter inert unless task-armed (bare test_helper wiring = one stderr notice, no-op); legacy artifacts rejected with a message naming mix spec.cover.test (decision 5); task name unchanged (decision 2); consumers = spec.triangle + spec.review only, spec.check never runs triangulation (decision 1); recommend OTP >= 27.2 for native, no hard gate (decision 4).

HONEST-LANGUAGE RULE (binding, from the 155.8 cycle): docs must never claim 'exact' per-test attribution — use observed/race-bounded language consistent with the aggregate_first ADR, referencing specled_-cpw (ExUnit cast race) and the file-level proxy reality (specled_-jjq) where per-test precision is discussed. Percent = covered-by-ANY-test; self-verified = composite verdict, never a per-spec percent.

Confirmed file paths for deliverable 1 (audit line numbers may have drifted — grep for 'Coverage.Formatter' and 'formatters:' in each): docs/adoption.md, skills/spec-led-bootstrap/SKILL.md, skills/spec-led-bootstrap/references/adoption-phases.md, skills/spec-led-bootstrap/references/task-templates.md, skills/spec-led-bootstrap/references/detection-matrix.md, priv/spec_init/agents/skills/spec-led-development/SKILL.md.eex. Sweep the WHOLE repo for the test_helper wiring instruction, not just this list.

Release notes = a 0.4.0 (Unreleased) section in CHANGELOG.md leading with the behavior breaks; do NOT bump VERSION (the release PR flow owns that).

The doc-identifier lint gate applies: every command, flag, finding code, module/function name, and path you write must resolve against the tree — validate by execution (run the commands with --help / real fixtures) wherever possible.

LESSONS:
- append new code below existing api_boundary-bound functions (specled_-wp7, from 155.1)
- mix compile --force before spec.check seeds hashes for brand-new bound functions (from 155.6)
- reconcile-ALL-claim-surfaces obligations need repo-wide greps incl. @doc strings and test names; disclose survivors, never claim completion past them (from 155.8)

## Allowed touches

- docs/** (adoption.md, concepts.md, NEW docs/coverage.md)
- README.md, CHANGELOG.md
- skills/spec-led-bootstrap/** (SKILL.md + references/*)
- priv/spec_init/agents/skills/spec-led-development/SKILL.md.eex
- priv/spec_init/workflows/spec_review.yml.eex
- .spec/specs/*.spec.md ONLY for gate-demanded co-change (docs subject bindings, if any)
- .spec/realization_hashes.json (tool-regenerated)

## Out of Scope (files)

- lib/**, test/** (no code or test changes — docs-only ticket)
- VERSION
- .spec/decisions/** (ADRs are complete; do not amend)

## Out of Scope (intent)

- No behavior changes anywhere
- No new claims beyond landed reality; no 'exact' attribution language
- No VERSION bump

## Verification

- Zero instances of the SpecLedEx.Coverage.Formatter test_helper wiring instruction anywhere in the repo (grep proof); replaced with the one-command guidance + 'never add the formatter anywhere — it is inert unless the task arms it'
- Zero remaining false 'mix spec.check runs triangulation' claims in docs/skills/scaffolds (grep proof); real consumers (spec.triangle, spec.review Coverage tab) named
- NEW docs/coverage.md covers: one-command entry; coveralls coexistence + mix spec.cover.ingest; what the numbers mean (ANY-test percent; self-verified composite); --per-test contract (serialized required, async fails, --allow-async degrades, race-bounded attribution language, cost model); OTP posture; prerequisites (tracer manifest); artifact hygiene (.gitignore .spec/_coverage/ + cover/, generated_at staleness); full refusal-reason catalogue with fixes
- README command table lists both coverage commands, links docs/coverage.md
- spec_review.yml.eex runs mix spec.cover.test before mix spec.review
- CHANGELOG 0.4.0 section leads with behavior breaks (test_helper wiring no-ops loudly; default mode changed, name kept; async under --per-test fails; legacy artifacts rejected)
- Phase-4 detection heuristic updated (artifact-exists / CI-runs-cover, not grep-for-Formatter)
- Every documented command/flag/path validated by execution; doc-identifier lint green
- mix compile --warnings-as-errors, full mix spec.check, format all green

comments.text:
IMPLEMENTED
Session: impl-1
Log: /Users/mzubieta/.local/state/agentic-runs/specled_ex/specled_-155.9/codex-impl-1/log

Commit: af16144
Files touched: .spec/specs/package.spec.md (surface: +docs/coverage.md, gate-demanded co-change), CHANGELOG.md, README.md, docs/adoption.md, docs/concepts.md, docs/coverage.md (new), priv/spec_init/agents/skills/spec-led-development/SKILL.md.eex, priv/spec_init/workflows/spec_review.yml.eex, skills/spec-led-bootstrap/SKILL.md, skills/spec-led-bootstrap/references/{adoption-phases,configurable-levers,detection-matrix,task-templates}.md

Grep proofs:
1) test_helper wiring instructions anywhere in repo (excl lib/, test/, deps/, _build/):
   grep -rn "ExUnit\.start(formatters:.*Coverage\.Formatter\|SpecLedEx\.Coverage\.Formatter.*test_helper\|Wire.*per-test coverage into the test helper" . --include="*.md" --include="*.eex" --include="*.ex" | grep -v '^\./deps/\|^\./_build/\|^\./lib/\|^\./test/'
   -> 4 hits, all corrective/documentary (CHANGELOG describing the fix, docs/coverage.md's refusal-catalogue row explaining the no-op, skills/spec-led-bootstrap/SKILL.md's "never grep for this" note, lib/specled_ex/coverage/formatter.ex's own moduledoc — code file, out of scope). Zero "do this" instructions remain.
2) False "spec.check runs/gates triangulation" claims:
   grep -rn "spec\.check.*\(untested_realization\|untethered_test\|underspecified_realization\)\|\(untested_realization\|untethered_test\|underspecified_realization\).*spec\.check" . --include="*.md" --include="*.eex" | grep -v '^\./deps/\|^\./_build/\|^\./\.spec/'
   -> 1 hit: docs/adoption.md:160 "`mix spec.check` itself never runs triangulation" — the corrected text itself, not a leftover false claim.
3) "exact" per-test attribution language in anything authored this ticket:
   grep -rn "exact.*per-test\|per-test.*exact attribution\|exactly which test" docs/coverage.md docs/adoption.md docs/concepts.md README.md skills/spec-led-bootstrap/ priv/spec_init/agents/skills/spec-led-development/SKILL.md.eex priv/spec_init/workflows/spec_review.yml.eex CHANGELOG.md
   -> 0 hits.

Additional finding beyond the ticket's named surfaces, fixed under the same deliverable-2 mandate: `.spec/config.yml`'s `branch_guard.severities` for `branch_guard_untested_realization`/`branch_guard_untethered_test`/`branch_guard_underspecified_realization` is dead configuration — confirmed by reading source (zero calls to `Severity.resolve/3` from spec.triangle.ex, coverage_triangulation.ex, review.ex, review/coverage_closure.ex; severities are hardcoded in coverage_triangulation.ex). Every place across adoption.md, the bootstrap skill, and the scaffolded skill that told adopters to configure these codes' severity (as a "graduate" or "permanent opt-out" lever) has been corrected to state plainly that there is nothing to configure — they are fixed-severity mix spec.triangle/mix spec.review diagnostics, never part of the mix spec.check gate.

Claims validated by execution (real commands run against scratch fixtures at /private/tmp/.../scratchpad/cov_fixture, cov_empty, cov_real_dep while writing docs/coverage.md):
- `mix spec.cover.test` default aggregate mode: real success message ("ingested 1 files, 3 mfas into ...") and empty-coverage refusal message, both from actual runs.
- `mix spec.cover.test --per-test`: clean run, async-contamination refusal message, `--allow-async` degrade warning — all captured verbatim from real output.
- `mix spec.cover.ingest`: success message, empty-coverage refusal, no-args usage error — all from real runs.
- Legacy-artifact and invalid-artifact rejection messages: captured via `SpecLedEx.Coverage.Store.read_v2/1` against a hand-written v1 artifact and garbage bytes.
- The central claim behind deliverable 1 — bare-wiring the formatter into `test_helper.exs` under plain `mix test` is an inert no-op — reproduced against a fixture with `spec_led_ex` as a REAL path dependency (not the internal ERL_LIBS test-harness trick), confirming the exact stderr notice and confirming no artifact is written.
- Tracer-manifest prerequisite and `generated_at` staleness claims verified by reading `lib/mix/tasks/spec.triangle.ex` (`Tracer.manifest_path/0`, "note: tracer manifest missing; closure walk skipped") and `lib/specled_ex/review/html.ex` (`@coverage_stale_after_seconds 24 * 60 * 60`) directly, since these aren't triggerable via a one-shot fixture run.
- `mix spec.check` never touching `CoverageTriangulation` confirmed by `grep -c "CoverageTriangulation" lib/mix/tasks/spec.check.ex` = 0, versus real references in spec.triangle.ex and review/coverage_closure.ex.

Verification tier: full-system — mix compile --force --warnings-as-errors (clean), mix format --check-formatted (clean), mix test test/docs_identifier_lint_test.exs (2 tests, 0 failures), mix spec.check --no-run-commands then full mix spec.check (both exit 0; the sole finding is a pre-existing :info branch_guard_missing_subject_update on lib/specled_ex/coverage.ex from an earlier epic ticket already on this branch, unrelated to this change).
Final-tier runs: 1 (green on first run; no finals_gt1).
Deferred items: none — this is the final ticket of epic specled_-155 and every named deliverable plus the orchestrator addenda was completed in this pass.

Summary: Removed every test_helper.exs formatter-wiring instruction repo-wide and every false "spec.check runs triangulation" claim, replacing both with landed reality (aggregate-first default, mix spec.triangle/mix spec.review as the sole triangulation consumers). Shipped docs/coverage.md as the adopter-facing coverage contract with every claim validated by execution, wired README's command table and the CI scaffold, and led the CHANGELOG 0.4.0 section with the behavior breaks.

VERIFY: PASS
Session: verify-1
Log: /Users/mzubieta/.local/state/agentic-runs/specled_ex/specled_-155.9/codex-verify-1/log

Mechanical checks:
- Files touched: 13
- Files outside Allowed globs: 0
- Files matching Out-of-Scope globs: 0 (lib/**, test/**, VERSION, .spec/decisions/** all UNTOUCHED, confirmed; package.spec.md +1 surface line is gate-demanded co-change, allowed)

Semantic checks:
- Grep proofs re-run: (a) Coverage.Formatter — PASS, every doc/skill/scaffold hit is 'never add / inert unless armed' guidance or CHANGELOG-describing-the-fix, zero wiring instructions; (b) spec.check<->triangulation — PASS, 5 hits all honest negations; (c) exact per-test/attribution — PASS, 1 hit is CHANGELOG honest negation ('never "exact"').
- docs/coverage.md execution validation: mix help spec.cover.test + spec.cover.ingest — every documented flag (--per-test, --allow-async, --output) exists. read_v2 reproduced LIVE: legacy -> {:error,:legacy_artifact,msg} exact match, garbage -> {:error,:invalid_artifact} exact. success/ingest-Wrote/empty-coverage/async-refusal/degraded/formatter-disabled/no-hits/externally-harvested messages all quote-matched verbatim to source. .gitignore /cover/ + /.spec/_coverage/ match verbatim. OTP recommend >=27.2 no-gate matches Snapshot.runtime_mode/0 + ADR. spec.cover.test ran the suite live (exported cover). Cold direct invocation in the specled repo root hits a MixRuntime.ensure_started! yaml_elixir loadpaths quirk (lib/ code, out of scope, does not affect adopter usage) — not a doc defect.
- Severity-config code claim: TRUE. Severity.resolve/3 callers = branch_check.ex, config/prose.ex, trailer.ex only; the 3 triangulation codes are hardcoded in lib/specled_ex/coverage_triangulation.ex (severity strings), consumed by spec.triangle.ex + review/coverage_closure.ex, and absent from lib/specled_ex/config/ and lib/specled_ex/branch_check/. branch_guard.severities has no effect on them. All doc corrections consistent; old configure/graduate/off guidance fully removed. spec_triangulation: :indirect honored at coverage_triangulation.ex:299-301.
- CHANGELOG accuracy: 0.4.0 (Unreleased); all 4 breaks verified true (default mode changed + name kept per decision 2; test_helper wiring no-ops loudly; async fails under --per-test; legacy artifacts rejected per decision 5). VERSION untouched (0.3.1).
- spec_review.yml.eex + README: template runs mix spec.cover.test BEFORE mix spec.review, EEx compiles; README Coverage Commands lists both commands and links docs/coverage.md (resolves).
- Whole-file corpus findings: none. mix spec.triangle --all valid; all commands/flags/codes/paths resolve.
- Spec stubs run: spec.check x2 stable (exit 0, status=pass errors=0 warnings=0, findings=1 pre-existing :info), tree clean; doc-identifier lint 2 tests 0 failures.
- Targeted verification: pass — compile --force --warnings-as-errors clean; format --check-formatted exit 0.
- Full-suite verification: not run by verifier (by design)
- Audit-expanded scope: none

Verdict: PASS

Notes: docs/coverage.md legacy refusal row renders ' -- ' where the source message uses an em-dash ' — '; cosmetic only, the unique message stem 'Legacy per-test coverage artifact detected' matches verbatim. Advisory, not a Gap.

title: T9: Docs + onboarding sweep, docs/coverage.md, workflow template, release notes
